tool.name: MAGs-visualization drep-cluster-func
tool.description: Create a dRep cluster plot with functional annotation
tool.command:
mags-visualization drep-cluster-func --drep '$drep' --gtdb '$gtdb' --pathways '$pathways' --top_n '$top_n' --tax_levels_space '$tax_levels_space' --format '$format' --mode '$mode' --module_label '$module_label'
#if $module_selection.selection_mode == "top" --top_modules "$module_selection.top_modules" #else --top_modules None #end if
#if $tax_levels --tax_levels #for $tl in $tax_levels '$tl' #end for #end if
@FIG_SIZE_COMMAND@
-o outputs && #if $mode == 'mean' mv outputs/drep_cluster_functional_core_*.${format} outputs/final_plot_core.png #elif $mode == 'variance' mv outputs/drep_cluster_functional_difference_*.${format} outputs/final_plot_difference.png #else mv outputs/drep_cluster_functional_core_*.${format} outputs/final_plot_core.png && mv outputs/drep_cluster_functional_difference_*.${format} outputs/final_plot_difference.png #end if

param.label: dRep cluster table
param.help: Use the unmodified dRep cluster table, typically the Cdb.csv output. The table must contain 'genome' and 'secondary_cluster'; each row assigns one genome to one dRep cluster.

param.label: GTDB annotation table
param.help: Use the unmodified GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing 'user_genome' and 'classification'. The representative genome taxonomy is shown next to each cluster bar.

param.label: Pathway completeness table
param.help: Use a pathway/module completeness table, for example kegg_pathway_completeness.tsv, containing 'contig', 'module_accession', 'completeness', 'pathway_name', and 'pathway_class'. Completeness values are used for the functional module annotation.

param.label: Taxonomy levels
param.help: Taxonomic ranks from the GTDB classification to display beside each dRep cluster, for example phylum and genus.

param.label: Module label style
param.help: Number of largest dRep species-level clusters to display, ranked by the number of member MAGs. The functional module completeness values are shown for these selected clusters.

param.label: Top N clusters
param.help: Number of largest dRep species-level clusters to display, ranked by the number of member MAGs. Each selected cluster is shown as a horizontal bar.

param.name: selection_mode
param.label: Module selection
param.help: Choose whether to show only the top-ranked functional modules or all modules from the pathway completeness table.

param.name: top_modules
param.label: Number of top modules
param.help: Number of functional modules to display after ranking by the selected mode: highest mean completeness for core modules, or highest variance for differential modules.

param.label: Spacing between taxonomy columns (cm))
param.help: Horizontal spacing between the taxonomy annotation columns in cm. Increase this value if taxonomy labels overlap.

param.label: Functional module selection mode
param.help: Select which functional module plot(s) to create: 'mean' highlights core modules with high average completeness across clusters, 'variance' highlights modules that differ most between clusters, and 'both' creates both outputs.

tool.help:
**MAGs-visualization: drep-cluster-func**
This tool creates functional annotation plots for the top N dRep species-level cluster. Clusters are selected by the number of member MAGs and annotated with GTDB taxonomy and KEGG module/pathway completeness values.
Functional modules can be selected using different modes:
- **mean**: highlights core functional modules with high average completeness across clusters - **variance**: highlights modules that vary most between clusters - **both**: generates both plots for comparison
The module selection option allows either displaying the top-ranked modules for the selected mode or all available modules.
**Input files**
- **dRep cluster table** (``--drep``): use the unmodified dRep cluster table, typically the ``Cdb.csv`` output. The tool uses the ``genome`` and ``secondary_cluster`` columns to count how many MAGs are assigned to each cluster. Additional dRep columns can remain in the file and are ignored when they are not needed.
Example::
genome,secondary_cluster,threshold,cluster_method,comparison_algorithm,primary_cluster SRR24759596_bin_37640.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759600_bin_174663.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759601_bin_46010.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1
- **GTDB-Tk annotation table** (``--gtdb``): use an unmodified GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing ``user_genome`` and ``classification``. Genome identifiers are normalized internally before matching them to the dRep and pathway completeness tables. The example below shows the relevant columns only; the original GTDB-Tk output may contain additional columns and can be used unmodified.
Example with the relevant columns shown::
user_genome classification SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Frischella;s__Frischella perrara SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Gilliamella;s__Gilliamella sp945271295
- **KEGG pathway completeness table** (``--pathways``): use a pathway/module completeness table, for example ``kegg_pathway_completeness.tsv``. The table must contain ``contig``, ``module_accession``, ``completeness``, ``pathway_name``, and ``pathway_class``. The tool uses ``contig`` to match MAGs/genomes, ``module_accession`` and ``pathway_name`` for module labels, ``pathway_class`` for grouping, and ``completeness`` as the value shown in the functional annotation.
Example::
contig module_accession completeness pathway_name pathway_class matching_ko missing_ko SRR24759597_bin_27614.fasta M00001 100.0 Glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof pathway), glucose => pyruvate Pathway modules; Carbohydrate metabolism; Central carbohydrate metabolism K00134,K00845,K00850,K00873,K00927,K01624,K01689,K01803,K01810,K01834 SRR24759597_bin_27614.fasta M00002 100.0 Glycolysis, core module involving three-carbon compounds Pathway modules; Carbohydrate metabolism; Central carbohydrate metabolism K00134,K00873,K00927,K01689,K01803,K01834
**Parameters**
- **Taxonomy levels**: GTDB ranks shown beside each dRep cluster, for example phylum and genus. - **Module label style**: controls whether KEGG modules are labeled by accession, name, or both. - **Top N clusters**: number of largest dRep clusters to show, ranked by MAG count. - **Module selection**: choose whether to display top-ranked modules or all modules from the pathway completeness table. - **Number of top modules**: number of modules shown when top-module selection is enabled. - **Functional module selection mode**: ``mean`` creates the core-module plot, ``variance`` creates the differential-module plot, and ``both`` creates both outputs. - **Spacing between taxonomy columns**: horizontal spacing between taxonomy labels in **cm**; increase this value if labels overlap.
**Output**
- Cluster plots with functional module annotation
**Layout options:** All size and spacing parameters (figure width, height, spacers) are specified in **cm**.
Example output plots are available in the MAGs-visualization use cases with example configurations and parameter choices: https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases
For a direct example (bee microbiome dataset): https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases/bee-use-case/plots
